video_title: A Deadly American Marriage | Official Trailer | Netflix
reply_text: It was actually the prosecution that screwed up, not the law. Basically, the children were interviewed early on in the investigation and were clearly coached into making specific statements about their dad and how abusive he was to Molly. Kids come forward some time later and tell the investigators this, and the prosecution made the case that the interviews with the kids should be inadmissible. Judge agreed. Big mistake. If those recordings had been played to the jury, the children could've testified that they were coached and child psychology experts would've easily backed that up, considering the kids were using language that kids of that age group don't use. But because the tapes weren't submitted as evidence, the defense were able to use them for the appeals case on the grounds that it was the one bit of evidence that corroborated Molly's version of events. But because they were no longer in trial, there was no back and forth argument between the prosecution and defense about the coached statements, just that a key part of evidence had been deliberately excluded. Court of Appeals agreed. The prosecution then agreed on a plea deal instead of a full re-trial, which led to reduced charges and sentences. Hence why they are out.
